author: Dr. Lizard
title: How does WHO-5 classify MDS cases with ring sideroblasts but no SF3B1 mutation?
answer: WHO-5 allows MDS-SF3B1 diagnosis with 15% or more ring sideroblasts even without SF3B1 mutation.
detail: This represents a morphology-based pathway to the SF3B1 category in WHO-5 classification.
ICC does not allow this morphology-only pathway and requires the SF3B1 mutation itself.
Cases with ring sideroblasts but no SF3B1 mutation become MDS NOS in ICC classification.
The 15% ring sideroblast threshold is specific to WHO-5 for this diagnostic pathway.
releaseNote: WHO-5 allows MDS-SF3B1 diagnosis with 15% or more ring sideroblasts even without SF3B1 mutation, whereas ICC requires the mutation itself.
